mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS reached 138780 thousand in June 2014, reflecting steady job creation
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or below 0.25% signals persistent monetary accommodation that compresses risk-free rates and lifts S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS stood at 0.1% in June 2014, consistent with zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly different from zero compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and retail sales indicates broad demand-supply alignment where output growth is absorbed by consumer spending, amplifying earnings revisions and driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 439891 million in June 2014 and INDPRO was 103.6585 in May 2014
failure_condition: The joint signal from INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis low inflation environment, year-over-year increases in CPIAUCSL alleviate deflation fears and support nominal revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns while inflation remains below 3%.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 237.083 in May 2014, indicating subdued but positive inflation
failure_condition: During regimes where CPIAUCSL year-over-year change is between 1% and 2.5%, positive S&P 500 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts lead the business cycle; an increase in HOUST signals construction sector momentum and downstream demand for durable goods, predicting positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1001 thousand units in May 2014, showing recovery in residential construction
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes fail to exhibit positive correlation with S&P 500 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening that raises wage income and consumption, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell to 6.1% in June 2014 from 6.7% in March 2014, a 0.6pp decline
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following a 0.3pp decline in UNRATE over 3 months are not positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects manufacturing sector health and capacity utilization; month-over-month increases in INDPRO predict positive near-term equity returns through earnings momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO stood at 103.6585 in May 2014, indicating expanding industrial output
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes fail to show positive correlation with S&P 500 5-day forward returns, yielding Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with subdued CPI inflation indicate real demand expansion without imminent monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 439891 million in June 2014 alongside CPIAUCSL at 237.083 in May 2014 shows strong real demand amid low inflation
failure_condition: The interaction of RSAFS growth and subdued CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict 60-day S&P 500 returns, with interaction term p-value above 0.05 over a 3-year window.
